pair_1_verus_strategies.conclusion: Model has strong Verus strategy knowledge. Correctly identified pure_smt for A and by(bit_vector) + assert forall for B. No issues with Verus-specific constructs.

pair_2_vstd_discovery.conclusion: Strong vstd lemma discovery. Found lemma_mul_mod_noop_right/left, lemma_mul_is_distributive_add, lemma_add_mod_noop, lemma_mul_is_associative by name. Pattern from C (mod-noop + distributivity) transferred directly to D (mod-noop + associativity).

pair_3_composition.conclusion: Model can compose 2-3 sub-lemmas effectively. E needed 2 lemmas (u64_5_as_nat_add + add_mod_noop), F needed 5 lemmas (p_gt_2, pow255_gt_19, mod_pos_bound, small_mod, mod_twice) across different files. Both passed on attempt 1.

pair_4_computation.conclusion: Model handles both by(compute) and by(bit_vector) strategies. G used by(compute) for constant arithmetic. H initially failed because by(bit_vector) cannot handle symbolic array indexing — needed let-bindings to extract elements first. This is a Verus-specific gotcha learned on attempt 2.

pair_5_scaling.conclusion: Complexity cliff hits at ~55 LOC. Both I (55 LOC GT) and J (93 LOC GT) failed. The fundamental bottleneck in I was bridging flat sum definitions with recursive bytes_as_nat_prefix — the SMT solver cannot match these without reveal_with_fuel or explicit bridge lemmas. J failed on type issues with macro-generated lemma signatures and incomplete algebraic identity proof.

verus_pattern_knowledge.evidence: A: pure_smt correct, B: by(bit_vector)+assert forall correct, H: by(bit_vector) correct after learning array extraction gotcha, G: by(compute) correct
verus_pattern_knowledge.pipeline_action: No special tooling needed. Model knows by(bit_vector), by(compute), assert forall |i| ... by { }, =~=, requires in by-blocks.

vstd_lemma_discovery.evidence: C: found lemma_mul_mod_noop_right, lemma_mul_is_distributive_add, lemma_add_mod_noop. D: found lemma_mul_mod_noop_left, lemma_mul_is_associative. E: found lemma_u64_5_as_nat_add. F: found lemma_small_mod, lemma_mod_twice, lemma_mod_pos_bound. All correct names and signatures.
vstd_lemma_discovery.pipeline_action: Current vstd knowledge is adequate for common arithmetic lemmas. May need RAG for less common lemmas.

sub_lemma_composition.evidence: E: composed 2 lemmas (passed). F: composed 5 lemmas across different files (passed). Up to ~20 LOC proofs with 3-5 sub-lemma calls are feasible.
sub_lemma_composition.pipeline_action: No special tooling needed for composition up to 5 lemma chains.

concrete_computation.evidence: G: by(compute) for constant arithmetic passed. H: by(bit_vector) for symbolic bounds passed after learning array extraction pattern.
concrete_computation.pipeline_action: Document the by(bit_vector) array indexing gotcha as a known pattern.

scaling.evidence: Holes A-H (5-34 LOC GT) all passed. Hole I (55 LOC GT) failed. Hole J (93 LOC GT) failed. The cliff is around 50 LOC. Failures were not about strategy or lemma knowledge but about connecting different representations (flat sums vs recursive specs) and managing 50+ lines of intermediate assertions.
scaling.pipeline_action: For proofs above ~50 LOC: (1) provide pre-decomposed proof sketches with intermediate goals, (2) provide bridge lemmas that connect flat and recursive representations, (3) consider proof decomposition into smaller sub-lemmas.

top_3_pipeline_priorities.justification: 2/2 failures (I, J) were due to proof complexity exceeding ~50 LOC. The model can handle individual steps but cannot produce 55-93 line proofs in one shot. Need automatic decomposition of large proofs into sub-lemma chains.

Hole I failed specifically because the SMT solver couldn't connect flat spec fn definitions (u8_32_as_nat) with recursive ones (bytes_as_nat_prefix). A library of bridge lemmas or reveal_with_fuel hints would unblock this class of proofs.

Hole H failed on attempt 1 due to by(bit_vector) not supporting symbolic array indexing. Hole J failed partly on macro-generated lemma type signatures. A database of Verus-specific gotchas (array extraction for bitvector, macro-generated function signatures, etc.) would prevent these first-attempt failures.

confidence_calibration.notes: Model was well-calibrated or underconfident on all holes. Pre-confidence of 2-3 on holes that passed first attempt (C, D, E, F) suggests the model underestimates its own ability for 10-20 LOC proofs. Confidence of 1-2 on failing holes (I, J) was accurate.
